skill.name: wifi-management
skill.description: Monitor, analyze, and optimize WiFi networks and client connections. Track signal strength, analyze channel usage, and optimize band distribution.
skill.summary: Analyze UniFi WiFi networks, clients, bands, channels, and access point performance.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 16 external command patterns and one weak cryptographic algorithm pattern. Review found the command alerts are Markdown-formatted UniFi tool names and workflow steps, and the crypto alert is a generic WiFi description. No evidence found of shell execution, weak cryptography instructions, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Tool Names Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The cited lines use backticks to format UniFi tool names and workflow steps in documentation, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited backtick text is inside Markdown bullets and numbered lists. No shell command, Ruby interpolation, subprocess call, or instruction to execute local commands is present.

low_findings.title: WiFi Description Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding is a false positive. The cited line describes WiFi monitoring and does not mention WEP, MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter description text for WiFi management. There is no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithm usage or guidance.

content.user_title: Optimize WiFi Networks with UniFi Insights
content.value_statement: WiFi teams need fast visibility into client health, access point load, and channel conditions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through UniFi WiFi analysis workflows.
actual_capabilities: Lists WiFi networks and reviews their status and configuration details.
Shows SSID broadcast information for UniFi wireless networks.
Reviews connected WiFi clients and their connection status.
Analyzes client statistics, signal strength, and band distribution.
Checks access point performance metrics for load and capacity planning.
Produces WiFi performance recommendations from available UniFi data.
limitations: Requires compatible UniFi network tools to provide live data.
Does not modify network settings unless separate tools allow changes.
Cannot measure physical coverage without device and site survey data.
Recommendations depend on the completeness of available client statistics.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot Slow WiFi
use_cases.description: Find weak signal clients, overloaded access points, and band imbalance during a support investigation.

use_cases.title: Review Wireless Capacity
use_cases.description: Compare client distribution and access point load before adding devices or expanding coverage.

use_cases.title: Plan Channel Improvements
use_cases.description: Analyze channel usage and interference indicators to prepare safer optimization recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Show WiFi Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Show all WiFi networks, SSID broadcasts, and connected client counts. Highlight anything offline or unusual.

prompt_templates.title: Find Weak Clients
prompt_templates.prompt: List WiFi clients with poor signal strength or unstable connections. Group them by network, band, and access point.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Band Balance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze 2.4GHz and 5GHz client distribution. Identify overloaded bands and recommend practical band steering changes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Optimization Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review WiFi networks, clients, broadcasts, and access point stats. Produce a prioritized optimization plan with evidence for each recommendation.

output_examples.input: Which clients have poor signal strength?
output_examples.output: A concise list of affected clients, their access points, signal quality, band, and suggested next checks.

output_examples.input: Analyze channel usage and suggest optimizations.
output_examples.output: A ranked summary of crowded channels, affected access points, and recommended channel planning actions.

output_examples.input: Compare WiFi performance across all APs.
output_examples.output: A comparison of access point load, connected clients, performance concerns, and capacity planning notes.

best_practices: Start with network and broadcast status before reviewing individual clients.
Compare signal strength, band, and access point load together.
Treat recommendations as change plans that require operator approval.
anti_patterns: Do not assume every slow client has a WiFi issue.
Do not change channels without checking nearby access points first.
Do not ignore 2.4GHz clients when evaluating band balance.

faq.question: Does this skill change WiFi settings?
faq.answer: No. It describes monitoring and analysis workflows. Configuration changes require separate authorized tools.

faq.question: Which UniFi data does it use?
faq.answer: It references WiFi networks, broadcasts, network clients, client statistics, and access point performance metrics.

faq.question: Can it identify weak signal clients?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides analysis of client statistics and signal strength when that data is available.

faq.question: Can it recommend channel changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can recommend channel and band adjustments based on available performance and usage data.

faq.question: Is live network access required?
faq.answer: Yes. Live findings require connected UniFi tools that can retrieve current network data.

faq.question: Does it support capacity planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps review access point performance and client distribution for planning decisions.
